# FanOps — Architecture & Implementation Governance

**Source fingerprint `fb73340f7732bb43` · generator `arch/1.0.0`**

Regenerate: `python -m tools.arch regen`

> This document is a **view**. It is generated from the machine-readable artifacts in `.reports/architecture/derived/` and `.reports/architecture/governance/`. It contains no hand-written facts.

## 1. The architecture, measured

| | |
|---|---|
| Modules | **134** |
| Compile-time import edges (G1) | **564** |
| Lazy (in-function) edges | **337** |
| Typing-only edges | 7 |
| Non-trivial compile-time SCCs | **1** |
| Layer levels (G1c, the SCC-condensation) | **11** |
| Lazy edges strictly UPWARD (layering inversions) | **59** |
| Lazy edges lateral (same level — *not* inversions) | 53 |
| Largest G2 potential-dependency SCC | 48 modules |
| Environment variables read | **73** |
| Route endpoints (method × path) | **150** (109 mutating) |
| CLI verbs | 63 unique (68 `add_parser` sites) |

### Metric definitions — because a derived number inherits the soundness of its definition

- **`compile_edges_G1`** — unique (source, target) pairs where the import is at MODULE LEVEL. `from P import n` yields an edge to P (P/__init__.py must execute) AND to P.n when n is itself a module.
- **`lazy_edges`** — unique (source, target) pairs where the import sits inside a FunctionDef. Materializes only if the function is CALLED.
- **`lazy_edges_lateral`** — lazy edges where level(target) == level(source). NOT inversions.
- **`lazy_edges_strictly_upward`** — lazy edges where level(target) > level(source) — TRUE layering inversions, legal ONLY because deferred to call time.
- **`level`** — longest-path depth over the SCC-CONDENSATION of G1 (a DAG by construction). level(X)=0 if X imports nothing internal; else 1+max(level(targets)).

- **`cli_add_parser_sites`** — add_parser() call sites — higher, because nested subparsers (e.g. `recover audit`) register additional verbs. Cycle 5's `verbs_total: 59` is THIS metric, while its enumerated list is the 54.
- **`cli_verbs_unique`** — distinct verb names.
- **`route_endpoints`** — one per (method, path) — the Flask unit. `/golive/live` has a GET page AND a POST that flips the system to LIVE PUBLISHING; keying on PATH collapses them into one, which is why the endpoint is canonical.
- **`route_unique_paths`** — distinct URL paths. Cycle 5's '149 routes' is THIS metric.

## 2. The subsystem partition

**Totality: ✅ TOTAL** — 134/134 modules assigned, 0 unassigned, 0 ghosts.

> The partition is an **analytic overlay** (Cycle 5). Nothing in the code enforces it. The module set is a **fact**; the grouping is a **model**. Totality is therefore a checkable property, and rule `ARCH-001` checks it.

> meta_graph uses an INJECTABLE `get` (so tests never touch the network). It is a real network seam and a literal `requests.*` census does NOT see it. Recorded here so the census is not mistaken for the whole truth.

## 4. The ratchets — mechanically enforced in CI today

- `cli.py` `print()` count — **declared by the test: 165** · **measured: 168** (an *exact-equality* budget shared by three slices)
- Zero-`print()` modules: 9
- Silent-broad-`except` baseline: 49 files

> ⚠️ **The swallow ratchet carries unclaimed slack.** These files declare a higher baseline than they currently use, so a future change can re-add a silent handler without CI noticing:

> - `src/fanops/pipeline.py` — baseline **13**, actual **12**
> - `src/fanops/post/zernio.py` — baseline **3**, actual **2**
> - `src/fanops/studio/views.py` — baseline **13**, actual **12**

## 5. The implementation contract, resolved against the code

- Slices: **12** · files under ownership: **19**
- Ordering DAG acyclic: **✅ PROVEN** (4 ordering edges, 2 co-requirements)

> A **co-requirement is not an ordering edge**. Modelling it as one would manufacture a DAG cycle that does not exist — the `C5-SC-2` error, applied to the implementation graph.

## 6. The executable policy set

Every rule is a predicate a machine evaluates. `python -m tools.arch check`.

| Rule | Severity | Title | Enforcement |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ARCH-001` | BLOCKING | The subsystem partition is TOTAL | derived module set == declared partition domain |
| `ARCH-002` | BLOCKING | No ghost modules | declared partition domain ⊆ derived module set |
| `ARCH-003` | BLOCKING | Every environment variable read is declared | derived env-read set ⊆ declared env set |
| `ARCH-004` | BLOCKING | No new compile-time import cycle | the set of non-trivial G1 SCCs must equal the approved set |
| `ARCH-005` | BLOCKING | UNKNOWNs cannot grow without approval | open unknown count <= approved ceiling |
| `ARCH-006` | BLOCKING | Generated artifacts are never hand-edited | regeneration is byte-identical to the committed bytes (drift.stale_artifacts for derived/, drift.stale_docs for the generated doc — the doc is as generated as the JSON but does not live in derived/, so it needs its own comparison or it drifts unwatched) |
| `ARCH-007` | BLOCKING | A lazy import may not be hoisted to module level (GB-1) | no edge pinned as must-stay-lazy may appear in the COMPILE graph |
| `ARCH-008` | WARNING | Every side effect is registered | derived side-effect census matches the declared totals |
| `ARCH-009` | BLOCKING | A derived numeric claim in a DECLARED artifact must match the derived fact | every cross-checked numeric claim equals its derived counterpart |
| `ARCH-010` | INFO | Unsupported constructs are recorded, never omitted | every construct the extractor cannot resolve is enumerated with evidence |
| `GOV-001` | BLOCKING | The canonical artifacts must be PRESENT | every canonical artifact this policy set reads must exist; a missing one is a FAILURE, never a skip |
| `IMPL-001` | BLOCKING | A slice owns only the files the contract grants it | every changed file in a slice's diff appears in that slice's allowance |
| `IMPL-002` | WARNING | A slice boundary must be a machine-checkable predicate, not prose | every permitted_functions entry resolves to a function that exists (or is marked planned) |
| `IMPL-003` | BLOCKING | The implementation DAG is acyclic | Tarjan over the ordering edges returns only singleton SCCs |
| `IMPL-004` | BLOCKING | No orphaned root cause | every root cause maps to >=1 slice, or to a recorded human decision to defer |
| `IMPL-005` | BLOCKING | Every slice has a rollback class and a verification set | every non-blocked slice appears in both matrices |
| `IMPL-006` | BLOCKING | Required verification cannot disappear | every INVARIANT test named by the matrix still exists once its slice is merged |
| `IMPL-007` | BLOCKING | The ratchet budgets the contract COPIES must match the tests that ENFORCE them | contract's declared ratchet numbers == the numbers in the CI test files |
| `IMPL-008` | BLOCKING | A slice must trace to an approved root cause | slice.root_causes is non-empty, or slice.status is PROPOSED/BLOCKED |
| `IMPL-009` | BLOCKING | No new unguarded door to a terminal Post state (GB-4) | the set of write sites to PostState.published/analyzed equals the approved guarded set |
| `IMPL-010` | BLOCKING | No ledger model may set extra='forbid' (GB-3) | no ConfigDict/model_config in models.py sets extra='forbid' |

#### `ARCH-001` — The subsystem partition is TOTAL

**Rationale.** kb/subsystems.json asserts every module is in exactly one subsystem. A module with no subsystem has no owner, no risk profile, and no reviewer — it is invisible to every claim the KB makes.

- **Scope:** `kb/subsystems.json vs derived/modules.json`
- **Severity:** BLOCKING
- **Enforcement:** derived module set == declared partition domain
- **Remediation:** Assign the module to a subsystem in kb/subsystems.json, then re-run the regeneration.
- **Exception process:** Add an entry to .reports/architecture/governance/exceptions.json with owner, justification, risk, mitigation, expiry and removal plan. An undocumented suppression is forbidden.

#### `ARCH-002` — No ghost modules

**Rationale.** A module the KB declares but which does not exist on disk means every downstream claim about it is about nothing.

- **Scope:** `kb/subsystems.json`
- **Severity:** BLOCKING
- **Enforcement:** declared partition domain ⊆ derived module set
- **Remediation:** Remove the module from kb/subsystems.json.
- **Exception process:** Add an entry to .reports/architecture/governance/exceptions.json with owner, justification, risk, mitigation, expiry and removal plan. An undocumented suppression is forbidden.

#### `ARCH-003` — Every environment variable read is declared

**Rationale.** The env surface is a trust boundary and the operator is a documented hand-editor (AR-09). An undeclared var is an undocumented input to a live system.

- **Scope:** `kb/configuration.json vs derived/configuration.json`
- **Severity:** BLOCKING
- **Enforcement:** derived env-read set ⊆ declared env set
- **Remediation:** Add the variable to kb/configuration.json (and docs/CONFIG.md), or remove the read.
- **Exception process:** Add an entry to .reports/architecture/governance/exceptions.json with owner, justification, risk, mitigation, expiry and removal plan. An undocumented suppression is forbidden.

#### `ARCH-004` — No new compile-time import cycle

**Rationale.** A compile-time cycle is a HARD load-order constraint that can become an ImportError at process start. Exactly one exists today (personas ↔ persona_store ↔ persona_research, UNK-C5-1) and it is undefended. A second one must not appear silently.

- **Scope:** `derived/dependencies.json G1`
- **Severity:** BLOCKING
- **Enforcement:** the set of non-trivial G1 SCCs must equal the approved set
- **Remediation:** Break the cycle, or defer one import into a function body (and pin it in ARCH-007's baseline).
- **Exception process:** Add an entry to .reports/architecture/governance/exceptions.json with owner, justification, risk, mitigation, expiry and removal plan. An undocumented suppression is forbidden.

#### `ARCH-005` — UNKNOWNs cannot grow without approval

**Rationale.** Every UNKNOWN is tracked architectural debt. Silent growth converts an audit into a backlog.

- **Scope:** `governance/unknowns.json`
- **Severity:** BLOCKING
- **Enforcement:** open unknown count <= approved ceiling
- **Remediation:** Close the unknown, or raise the ceiling explicitly in governance/unknowns.json with a rationale.
- **Exception process:** Add an entry to .reports/architecture/governance/exceptions.json with owner, justification, risk, mitigation, expiry and removal plan. An undocumented suppression is forbidden.

#### `ARCH-006` — Generated artifacts are never hand-edited

**Rationale.** A generated file that has been hand-edited is a fork of the truth that regeneration will silently destroy — or worse, that nobody regenerates because the diff is noisy.

- **Scope:** `derived/** + docs/ARCHITECTURE_GOVERNANCE.md`
- **Severity:** BLOCKING
- **Enforcement:** regeneration is byte-identical to the committed bytes (drift.stale_artifacts for derived/, drift.stale_docs for the generated doc — the doc is as generated as the JSON but does not live in derived/, so it needs its own comparison or it drifts unwatched)
- **Remediation:** Re-run `python -m tools.arch regen && python -m tools.arch docs` and commit. Never edit derived/ or the generated doc by hand.
- **Exception process:** Add an entry to .reports/architecture/governance/exceptions.json with owner, justification, risk, mitigation, expiry and removal plan. An undocumented suppression is forbidden.

#### `ARCH-007` — A lazy import may not be hoisted to module level (GB-1)

**Rationale.** Many lazy (in-function) import edges point to an equal-or-higher layer level, and dozens are STRICTLY UPWARD. The layered DAG holds ONLY because those imports are deferred to call time — a low, heavily-depended-on module like `config` reaches UP to `accounts`. Hoisting any one LOOKS LIKE A CLEANUP and can break the process at start. (The exact counts live in derived/dependencies.json; a number copied into this prose is the very defect this system exists to catch, so none is written here.)

- **Scope:** `governance/layering_baseline.json vs derived/dependencies.json`
- **Severity:** BLOCKING
- **Enforcement:** no edge pinned as must-stay-lazy may appear in the COMPILE graph
- **Remediation:** Keep the import inside the function body. If the hoist is genuinely correct, accept a new baseline deliberately: `python -m tools.arch baseline --accept` (a reviewed change).
- **Exception process:** Add an entry to .reports/architecture/governance/exceptions.json with owner, justification, risk, mitigation, expiry and removal plan. An undocumented suppression is forbidden.

#### `ARCH-008` — Every side effect is registered

**Rationale.** Subprocess, network, ledger-transaction, lock, mkdtemp and env-write sites are the system's blast radius. An unregistered one is an effect nobody reviewed.

- **Scope:** `kb/side_effects.json vs derived/side_effects.json`
- **Severity:** WARNING
- **Enforcement:** derived side-effect census matches the declared totals
- **Remediation:** Update kb/side_effects.json to the derived counts, or remove the effect.
- **Exception process:** Add an entry to .reports/architecture/governance/exceptions.json with owner, justification, risk, mitigation, expiry and removal plan. An undocumented suppression is forbidden.

#### `ARCH-009` — A derived numeric claim in a DECLARED artifact must match the derived fact

**Rationale.** The repository's signature defect, found in every one of five audit cycles, is: THE DOC NAMES A MECHANISM THAT DOES NOT EXIST. A number copied from code into prose is that defect in its cheapest form. Implementation wins over prose.

- **Scope:** `kb/**, contract/** vs derived/**`
- **Severity:** BLOCKING
- **Enforcement:** every cross-checked numeric claim equals its derived counterpart
- **Remediation:** Regenerate and update the declared artifact. The CODE is authoritative.
- **Exception process:** Add an entry to .reports/architecture/governance/exceptions.json with owner, justification, risk, mitigation, expiry and removal plan. An undocumented suppression is forbidden.

#### `ARCH-010` — Unsupported constructs are recorded, never omitted

**Rationale.** A census is only as good as its query. An omitted construct is indistinguishable from an absent one — the failure that made Cycle 5 report 39 network sites when there were 15.

- **Scope:** `derived/unsupported.json`
- **Severity:** INFO
- **Enforcement:** every construct the extractor cannot resolve is enumerated with evidence
- **Remediation:** Extend the extractor, or accept the construct as an UNKNOWN in governance/unknowns.json.
- **Exception process:** Add an entry to .reports/architecture/governance/exceptions.json with owner, justification, risk, mitigation, expiry and removal plan. An undocumented suppression is forbidden.

#### `GOV-001` — The canonical artifacts must be PRESENT

**Rationale.** *** A GATE THAT PASSES BECAUSE ITS INPUTS ARE MISSING IS THE WORST KIND OF DECORATION: it is a green check that checks nothing, and it is indistinguishable from a real one. *** This is not hypothetical. `.reports/architecture/` was in .gitignore — the ENTIRE knowledge base of Cycles 1-6 was NOT IN THE REPOSITORY. CI would have found no kb/, no contract/, no derived/, silently skipped every check that reads them, and gone GREEN.

- **Scope:** `.reports/architecture/{kb,contract,governance,derived}`
- **Severity:** BLOCKING
- **Enforcement:** every canonical artifact this policy set reads must exist; a missing one is a FAILURE, never a skip
- **Remediation:** The artifacts are tracked. Restore them (`git checkout .reports/architecture/`), and confirm .gitignore still carries the `!.reports/architecture/` negation.
- **Exception process:** Add an entry to .reports/architecture/governance/exceptions.json with owner, justification, risk, mitigation, expiry and removal plan. An undocumented suppression is forbidden.

#### `IMPL-001` — A slice owns only the files the contract grants it

**Rationale.** Two slices editing one file with no declared partition is how one silently widens into the other. File-level ownership must be total and unambiguous.

- **Scope:** `contract/file_ownership.json`
- **Severity:** BLOCKING
- **Enforcement:** every changed file in a slice's diff appears in that slice's allowance
- **Remediation:** Add the file to the slice's allowance in file_ownership.json (a reviewed scope change), or take it out of the diff.
- **Exception process:** Add an entry to .reports/architecture/governance/exceptions.json with owner, justification, risk, mitigation, expiry and removal plan. An undocumented suppression is forbidden.

#### `IMPL-002` — A slice boundary must be a machine-checkable predicate, not prose

**Rationale.** `permitted_functions: ['the daemon tick loop (:1300-1313)']` cannot be enforced by any machine. A boundary that only a human can evaluate is enforced by attention, and this codebase has taught us exactly what attention is worth.

- **Scope:** `contract/file_ownership.json partitions`
- **Severity:** WARNING
- **Enforcement:** every permitted_functions entry resolves to a function that exists (or is marked planned)
- **Remediation:** Rewrite the entry as a bare function identifier. See the migration plan in the runbook.
- **Exception process:** Add an entry to .reports/architecture/governance/exceptions.json with owner, justification, risk, mitigation, expiry and removal plan. An undocumented suppression is forbidden.

#### `IMPL-003` — The implementation DAG is acyclic

**Rationale.** An ordering cycle makes the sequence unexecutable. Note that CO-REQUIREMENTS are NOT ordering edges — modelling them as such would MANUFACTURE a cycle that does not exist (the C5-SC-2 error, applied to the implementation graph).

- **Scope:** `contract/implementation_contract.json`
- **Severity:** BLOCKING
- **Enforcement:** Tarjan over the ordering edges returns only singleton SCCs
- **Remediation:** Remove the back edge, or re-model it as a co-requirement if that is what it is.
- **Exception process:** Add an entry to .reports/architecture/governance/exceptions.json with owner, justification, risk, mitigation, expiry and removal plan. An undocumented suppression is forbidden.

#### `IMPL-004` — No orphaned root cause

**Rationale.** Cycle 4 had ten root causes and ten slices and it LOOKED like a bijection. RC-4+RC-5 collapse into S01, so RC-9 mapped to NOTHING — deferred, then simply untracked. A deferral is not a discharge.

- **Scope:** `contract/traceability.json`
- **Severity:** BLOCKING
- **Enforcement:** every root cause maps to >=1 slice, or to a recorded human decision to defer
- **Remediation:** Add a slice (a GUARD slice suffices for an unreachable root cause), or record the deferral.
- **Exception process:** Add an entry to .reports/architecture/governance/exceptions.json with owner, justification, risk, mitigation, expiry and removal plan. An undocumented suppression is forbidden.

#### `IMPL-005` — Every slice has a rollback class and a verification set

**Rationale.** 'Revert' is not one thing. CODE_REVERSIBLE, DATA_IRREVERSIBLE and WORLD_IRREVERSIBLE are different promises, and two of this program's slices are not simply revertible.

- **Scope:** `contract/rollback_matrix.json, contract/verification_matrix.json`
- **Severity:** BLOCKING
- **Enforcement:** every non-blocked slice appears in both matrices
- **Remediation:** Add the slice's rollback class and its verification set.
- **Exception process:** Add an entry to .reports/architecture/governance/exceptions.json with owner, justification, risk, mitigation, expiry and removal plan. An undocumented suppression is forbidden.

#### `IMPL-006` — Required verification cannot disappear

**Rationale.** A test that vanishes takes its invariant with it, silently.

- **Scope:** `contract/verification_matrix.json`
- **Severity:** BLOCKING
- **Enforcement:** every INVARIANT test named by the matrix still exists once its slice is merged
- **Remediation:** Restore the test, or record its removal as an explicit contract change.
- **Exception process:** Add an entry to .reports/architecture/governance/exceptions.json with owner, justification, risk, mitigation, expiry and removal plan. An undocumented suppression is forbidden.

#### `IMPL-007` — The ratchet budgets the contract COPIES must match the tests that ENFORCE them

**Rationale.** The contract pins the cli.py print budget as a load-bearing, exact-equality budget shared across three slices. Its copy once went stale in a single commit while the enforcing test moved on — which is the whole reason this rule exists. The authoritative number lives in the CI test and in derived/ratchets.json; it is deliberately NOT written here as an assignment.

- **Scope:** `contract/implementation_contract.json GB-6 vs derived/ratchets.json`
- **Severity:** BLOCKING
- **Enforcement:** contract's declared ratchet numbers == the numbers in the CI test files
- **Remediation:** Update the contract's GB-6 block from derived/ratchets.json. The TEST is authoritative.
- **Exception process:** Add an entry to .reports/architecture/governance/exceptions.json with owner, justification, risk, mitigation, expiry and removal plan. An undocumented suppression is forbidden.

#### `IMPL-008` — A slice must trace to an approved root cause

**Rationale.** Every code modification must be traceable to an approved root cause, or it is a hidden scope expansion. S12 traces to AR-04, a RISK — which is why it is marked PROPOSED and gated on PD-5 rather than smuggled into the program.

- **Scope:** `contract/implementation_contract.json`
- **Severity:** BLOCKING
- **Enforcement:** slice.root_causes is non-empty, or slice.status is PROPOSED/BLOCKED
- **Remediation:** Add the root cause, or mark the slice PROPOSED and surface it as a product decision.
- **Exception process:** Add an entry to .reports/architecture/governance/exceptions.json with owner, justification, risk, mitigation, expiry and removal plan. An undocumented suppression is forbidden.

#### `IMPL-009` — No new unguarded door to a terminal Post state (GB-4)

**Rationale.** The R1 published-URL invariant fires at CONSTRUCTION only. `model_copy` and `setattr` both bypass it. Four manual call-site guards hold the line. A FIFTH door saves cleanly and then BRICKS THE NEXT Ledger.load — taking down the daemon and every Studio page at once.

- **Scope:** `src/fanops/**`
- **Severity:** BLOCKING
- **Enforcement:** the set of write sites to PostState.published/analyzed equals the approved guarded set
- **Remediation:** Add an explicit non-empty public_url guard at the call site, and pin it in the baseline.
- **Exception process:** Add an entry to .reports/architecture/governance/exceptions.json with owner, justification, risk, mitigation, expiry and removal plan. An undocumented suppression is forbidden.

#### `IMPL-010` — No ledger model may set extra='forbid' (GB-3)

**Rationale.** Forward-compat holds by pydantic's DEFAULT, not by declaration. Setting `forbid` — a change that LOOKS LIKE TIGHTENING — turns a forward-rolled ledger into a hard ControlFileError and bricks every reader.

- **Scope:** `src/fanops/models.py`
- **Severity:** BLOCKING
- **Enforcement:** no ConfigDict/model_config in models.py sets extra='forbid'
- **Remediation:** Remove the setting. Forward-compat is load-bearing (SHIM-005).
- **Exception process:** Add an entry to .reports/architecture/governance/exceptions.json with owner, justification, risk, mitigation, expiry and removal plan. An undocumented suppression is forbidden.

- Open unknowns: **8** / approved ceiling **8**

## 8. What the extractor cannot resolve

**3** construct(s). Recorded, never dropped — an omitted construct is indistinguishable from an absent one.

| Module | Line | Kind | Why |
|---|---|---|---|
| `fanops.config` | 1130 | `computed_env_key` | env key is not a string literal; the variable name is not statically known |
| `fanops.studio.actions_run` | 243 | `dynamic_import` | the import target is computed at runtime; no static edge is derivable |
| `fanops.studio.actions_run` | 263 | `dynamic_import` | the import target is computed at runtime; no static edge is derivable |
